definition: The Gabaix-Koijen hypothesis that $1 of flow moves aggregate market capitalization by $3-5, amplified by passive investing's price-insensitive nature. If correct, every historical base rate for equity cycle behavior may be obsolete due to procyclical amplification mechanics.
evidence: Gabaix-Koijen theoretical framework and empirical estimates
Passive share at ~55% of US equity AUM
open_questions: No definitive passive-driven crash has occurred for empirical validation
Whether active managers still set marginal prices sufficiently
Whether the hypothesis invalidates historical cycle base rates
